description: Fix with non-obvious reason → fix type + body explaining the why
staged_diff_excerpt:
diff --git a/vault/skills/process-inbox/scripts/scan_inbox.py b/vault/skills/process-inbox/scripts/scan_inbox.py
index 2b3a1c9..7f4e8d2 100755
--- a/vault/skills/process-inbox/scripts/scan_inbox.py
+++ b/vault/skills/process-inbox/scripts/scan_inbox.py
@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@ DAILY_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$")
 def parse_frontmatter(text):
     if not text.startswith("---"):
         return {}, text
-    end = text.find("\n---")
+    end = text.find("\n---", 3)
     if end == -1:
         return {}, text
recent_log: d2c8b1a refactor(process-inbox): add scan_inbox.py for structured inbox discovery
b9f3c0e feat(qr-code): add generate_qr.py to replace inline script generation
expected_behavior: Generates: fix(scan-inbox): stop frontmatter parser matching opening fence as closing delimiter. Body explains: find('\n---') without offset 3 matched the opening '---' line itself when file starts with '---\n', causing empty frontmatter parse. Type=fix. Scope=scan-inbox or process-inbox. Body present because the offset=3 trick is non-obvious without context.

staged_diff_excerpt:
diff --git a/literature/skills/lit-search/scripts/search.py b/literature/skills/lit-search/scripts/search.py
@@ -45,6 +45,18 @@ def search_openalex(query, limit=20):

+        return []
+    return [normalise(r) for r in results if r.get('title')]

diff --git a/vault/skills/galaxy/SKILL.md b/vault/skills/galaxy/SKILL.md

-Run scan_links.py on all 20-Sources/papers/ notes.

+python3 "$SCAN" --vault-dir "$VAULT_DIR"
recent_log: d2c8b1a refactor(process-inbox): add scan_inbox.py for structured inbox discovery
expected_behavior: Warns user that staged changes span two unrelated concerns (lit-search bug fix and galaxy SKILL.md update). Suggests git reset HEAD or git stash -p to split. Then generates best-effort single message covering both, e.g.: 'fix(lit-search): guard empty OpenAlex results; update galaxy script path'. Notes that message covers multiple concerns.

description: Diff >300 lines → skill uses --stat + first 50 lines only, still generates valid message
staged_diff_excerpt:
diff --git a/literature/skills/lit-review/scripts/prisma_prefilter.py b/literature/skills/lit-review/scripts/prisma_prefilter.py
new file mode 100755
--- /dev/null
+++ b/literature/skills/lit-review/scripts/prisma_prefilter.py
@@ -0,0 +1,312 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""PRISMA pre-filter: auto-exclude/include papers before Claude screening."""
+import argparse, json, re
+from pathlib import Path
+
+def title_keyword_match(title, keywords):
+    title_lower = title.lower()
+    return any(kw.lower() in title_lower for kw in keywords)
+
+def citation_tier(paper):
+    citations = paper.get('citations', 0)
+    age = paper.get('age_years', 0)
+    if citations > 100 and age > 5:
+        return 'auto_include'
+    if citations > 20 and age < 3:
+        return 'auto_include'
+    return None
+
+# ... [262 more lines truncated — diff too large]
recent_log: b9f3c0e feat(qr-code): add generate_qr.py to replace inline script generation
d2c8b1a refactor(process-inbox): add scan_inbox.py for structured inbox discovery
expected_behavior: Detects diff is >300 lines (312 in stat). Uses --stat + first 50 lines only (already truncated in input). Generates valid message from what it can see: feat(lit-review): add PRISMA pre-filter script for deterministic paper auto-include/exclude. Notes in output that diff was truncated and message is based on stat + excerpt.
